_**User**_

I am creating a reactnative app StreamList. It is a React Native OTT content discovery and watchlist app. It allows users to browse trending and top-rated movies, search for titles, view detailed content information, and manage a personal watchlist . Create below  project structure for that -  streamlist/
├── src/
│   ├── api/
│   │   ├── client.ts          # Central Axios instance with interceptors
│   │   ├── movies.ts          # All movie/show endpoint functions
│   │   └── types.ts           # TypeScript interfaces for all API responses
│   ├── components/
│   │   ├── common/            # Shared across screens (ContentCard, Badge, SkeletonCard, etc.)
│   │   └── [screen-name]/     # Screen-specific components
│   ├── hooks/
│   │   ├── useHome.ts
│   │   ├── useSearch.ts
│   │   └── useMovieDetail.ts
│   ├── navigation/
│   │   ├── RootNavigator.tsx  # Bottom tab navigator
│   │   └── types.ts           # Navigation param types
│   ├── screens/
│   │   ├── HomeScreen.tsx
│   │   ├── SearchScreen.tsx
│   │   ├── DetailScreen.tsx
│   │   ├── WatchlistScreen.tsx
│   │   └── ProfileScreen.tsx  # Placeholder only
│   ├── store/
│   │   └── watchlistStore.ts  # Zustand store with AsyncStorage persistence
│   ├── theme/
│   │   ├── colors.ts          # All color tokens
│   │   ├── typography.ts      # Font sizes, weights, line heights
│   │   └── spacing.ts         # Spacing scale
│   └── utils/
│       └── image.ts           # TMDB image URL helpers
├── .env                       # Never committed
├── .env.example               # Committed — placeholder values only
├── .gitignore
├── .cursor/rules              # Your cursor rules — committed
├── prompt-logs/               # SpecStory exports — committed
│   └── .gitkeep
├── docs/
│   └── ADR.md                 # Architectural Decisions Record
├── app.json
├── tsconfig.json              # Strict mode enabled
└── package.json    

Add all the dependency for bottom tab navigation, axios for API call.
I am defneing the color code which are used in overall application

Define all of these in src/theme/colors.ts:

export const colors = {
  // Surfaces — layered from deepest to brightest
  surface:                   '#131313',  // Main app background
  surface_container_lowest:  '#0E0E0E',  // Deepest recessed elements
  surface_container_low:     '#1C1B1B',  // Section groupings
  surface_container:         '#232323',  // Mid-level containers
  surface_container_high:    '#2A2A2A',  // Elevated sections
  surface_container_highest: '#353534',  // Cards, interactive elements
  surface_bright:            '#3A3939',  // Hover/pressed states

  // Primary accent (Coral-Red)
  primary:                   '#FFB3AE',  // Gradient start / light tint
  primary_container:         '#FF5351',  // Gradient end / strong accent
  secondary_container:       '#822625',  // Active chip state (deep red)

  // Text
  on_surface:                '#E5E2E1',  // Primary text — never use pure white
  on_surface_variant:        '#E4BDBA',  // Secondary metadata text

  // Utility
  outline_variant:           'rgba(255,255,255,0.15)', // Ghost borders (accessibility only)
};

Typography
Define in src/theme/typography.ts. Use Manrope for display and headlines, Inter for body and labels.

Token	Font	Size	Weight	Letter Spacing	Usage
display-lg	Manrope	56px	800	-0.02em	Hero titles
display-md	Manrope	40px	800	-0.02em	Screen titles (e.g. "My Watchlist")
headline-md	Manrope	28px	700	-0.01em	Section headers ("Trending Now")
title-lg	Manrope	20px	600	0	Card titles
title-sm	Inter	14px	600	0	Buttons, labels
body-md	Inter	14px	400	0	Synopsis, body copy
label-sm	Inter	12px	400	0	Metadata (year, genre, rating)

No &#96;src/&#96; directory, navigation, theme, or API layer exists.\n\n## Dependencies to Install\n\n**Runtime:**\n- &#96;@react-navigation/native&#96; -- core navigation\n- &#96;@react-navigation/bottom-tabs&#96; -- bottom tab navigator\n- &#96;react-native-screens&#96; -- native screen containers (required by React Navigation)\n- &#96;axios&#96; -- HTTP client\n- &#96;zustand&#96; -- lightweight state management\n- &#96;@react-native-async-storage/async-storage&#96; -- persistence layer for zustand watchlist store\n- &#96;react-native-vector-icons&#96; -- tab bar icons\n- &#96;react-native-config&#96; -- read &#96;.env&#96; variables at runtime\n\n**Dev:**\n- &#96;@types/react-native-vector-icons&#96; -- type definitions\n\n\u003e &#96;react-native-safe-area-context&#96; is already installed.\n\n**Fonts (Manrope + Inter):**\nReact Native 0.85 supports auto-linking custom fonts. We will add the font files under &#96;assets/fonts/&#96; and configure &#96;react-native.config.js&#96; to link them. The actual &#96;.ttf&#96; files need to be downloaded separately (Google Fonts); the plan creates the config and documents the step.\n\n## Files to Create\n\n### Theme ([&#96;src/theme/&#96;](src/theme/))\n\n| File | Content |\n|------|---------|\n| [&#96;src/theme/colors.ts&#96;](src/theme/colors.ts) | Full &#96;colors&#96; object exactly as specified by user |\n| [&#96;src/theme/typography.ts&#96;](src/theme/typography.ts) | Typography tokens using Manrope/Inter with specified sizes, weights, letter-spacing |\n| [&#96;src/theme/spacing.ts&#96;](src/theme/spacing.ts) | Spacing scale (4px base: 0, 4, 8, 12, 16, 20, 24, 32, 40, 48, 64) |\n\n### API Layer ([&#96;src/api/&#96;](src/api/))\n\n| File | Content |\n|------|---------|\n| [&#96;src/api/client.ts&#96;](src/api/client.ts) | Central Axios instance pointing at TMDB v3, auth interceptor reads API key from &#96;react-native-config&#96; |\n| [&#96;src/api/movies.ts&#96;](src/api/movies.ts) | Exported functions: &#96;getTrending&#96;, &#96;getTopRated&#96;, &#96;searchMovies&#96;, &#96;getMovieDetail&#96; -- all call &#96;client&#96; |\n| [&#96;src/api/types.ts&#96;](src/api/types.ts) | TS interfaces: &#96;Movie&#96;, &#96;MovieDetail&#96;, &#96;PaginatedResponse&#96;, &#96;Genre&#96; |\n\n### Hooks ([&#96;src/hooks/&#96;](src/hooks/))\n\n| File | Content |\n|------|---------|\n| [&#96;src/hooks/useHome.ts&#96;](src/hooks/useHome.ts) | Fetches trending + top-rated lists via &#96;api/movies.ts&#96; |\n| [&#96;src/hooks/useSearch.ts&#96;](src/hooks/useSearch.ts) | Debounced search query, calls &#96;searchMovies&#96; |\n| [&#96;src/hooks/useMovieDetail.ts&#96;](src/hooks/useMovieDetail.ts) | Fetches single movie detail by ID |\n\n### Navigation ([&#96;src/navigation/&#96;](src/navigation/))\n\n| File | Content |\n|------|---------|\n| [&#96;src/navigation/types.ts&#96;](src/navigation/types.ts) | &#96;RootTabParamList&#96; with Home, Search, Watchlist, Profile; &#96;DetailStackParamList&#96; with Detail screen params |\n| [&#96;src/navigation/RootNavigator.tsx&#96;](src/navigation/RootNavigator.tsx) | Bottom tab navigator with 4 tabs (Home, Search, Watchlist, Profile), uses theme colors for tab bar styling |\n\n### Screens ([&#96;src/screens/&#96;](src/screens/))\n\nEach screen is a minimal placeholder that imports its hook (where applicable) and renders a &#96;\u003cView\u003e&#96; with the screen name.\n\n- [&#96;src/screens/HomeScreen.tsx&#96;](src/screens/HomeScreen.tsx) -- uses &#96;useHome&#96;\n- [&#96;src/screens/SearchScreen.tsx&#96;](src/screens/SearchScreen.tsx) -- uses &#96;useSearch&#96;\n- [&#96;src/screens/DetailScreen.tsx&#96;](src/screens/DetailScreen.tsx) -- uses &#96;useMovieDetail&#96;\n- [&#96;src/screens/WatchlistScreen.tsx&#96;](src/screens/WatchlistScreen.tsx) -- reads from zustand store\n- [&#96;src/screens/ProfileScreen.tsx&#96;](src/screens/ProfileScreen.tsx) -- static placeholder\n\n### Components ([&#96;src/components/&#96;](src/components/))\n\n- [&#96;src/components/common/.gitkeep&#96;](src/components/common/.gitkeep) -- placeholder for shared components (ContentCard, Badge, SkeletonCard, etc.)\n\n### Store ([&#96;src/store/&#96;](src/store/))\n\n- [&#96;src/store/watchlistStore.ts&#96;](src/store/watchlistStore.ts) -- Zustand store with &#96;addToWatchlist&#96;, &#96;removeFromWatchlist&#96;, &#96;isInWatchlist&#96;; persisted via &#96;zustand/middleware&#96; + AsyncStorage\n\n### Utils ([&#96;src/utils/&#96;](src/utils/))\n\n- [&#96;src/utils/image.ts&#96;](src/utils/image.ts) -- &#96;getImageUrl(path, size)&#96; helper for TMDB image CDN (&#96;https://image.tmdb.org/t/p/&#96;)\n\n### Root Config Files\n\n- [&#96;.env.example&#96;](.env.example) -- &#96;TMDB_API_KEY=your_api_key_here&#96;\n- [&#96;docs/ADR.md&#96;](docs/ADR.md) -- Initial ADR documenting tech choices (React Navigation, Zustand, Axios, TMDB API)\n- [&#96;prompt-logs/.gitkeep&#96;](prompt-logs/.gitkeep) -- empty placeholder\n\n### Modified Files\n\n- [&#96;App.tsx&#96;](App.tsx) -- Replace template content with &#96;NavigationContainer&#96; wrapping &#96;RootNavigator&#96;\n- [&#96;package.json&#96;](package.json) -- Updated by npm install\n- [&#96;.gitignore&#96;](.gitignore) -- Add &#96;.env&#96; entry (if not already present)\n- [&#96;react-native.config.js&#96;](react-native.config.js) -- Add &#96;assets: ['./assets/fonts/']&#96; for custom font linking\n\n## Data Flow (Architecture)\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;mermaid\ngraph TD\n    Screen[\"Screen (e.g. HomeScreen)\"] --\u003e|calls| Hook[\"Hook (e.g. useHome)\"]\n    Hook --\u003e|calls| APIFn[\"API Function (movies.ts)\"]\n    APIFn --\u003e|uses| Client[\"Axios Instance (client.ts)\"]\n    Client --\u003e|HTTP| TMDB[\"TMDB API\"]\n    Screen --\u003e|reads/writes| Store[\"Zustand Store\"]\n    Store --\u003e|persists| AsyncStorage[\"AsyncStorage\"]\n    Screen --\u003e|uses| Theme[\"Theme (colors/typography/spacing)\"]\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## Key Decisions\n\n- **No inline &#96;useEffect&#96; + fetch in screens** -- all data fetching lives in hooks per workspace rule\n- **No direct Axios imports outside &#96;client.ts&#96;** -- all endpoint functions in &#96;movies.ts&#96; use the shared instance\n- **No hardcoded hex/pixel values in components** -- everything references theme tokens\n- **Zustand over Redux** -- minimal boilerplate for a single watchlist store\n- **&#96;react-native-config&#96;** over &#96;react-native-dotenv&#96; -- more reliable native env injection, widely used in RN CLI projects\n",
  "todos": [

All tasks are complete. Here's a summary of everything that was done:

---

### Dependencies Installed (runtime)
- `@react-navigation/native`, `@react-navigation/bottom-tabs`, `@react-navigation/native-stack` -- navigation
- `react-native-screens` -- native screen containers
- `axios` -- HTTP client
- `zustand` -- state management
- `@react-native-async-storage/async-storage` -- persistence
- `react-native-vector-icons` -- tab bar icons
- `react-native-config` -- env variable injection

### Dependencies Installed (dev)
- `@types/react-native-vector-icons`

### Files Created (19 new files)

| Path | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `src/theme/colors.ts` | All color tokens (exact values from your spec) |
| `src/theme/typography.ts` | 7 typography tokens (Manrope + Inter, exact sizes/weights) |
| `src/theme/spacing.ts` | 11-step spacing scale (4px base) |
| `src/api/client.ts` | Central Axios instance with TMDB base URL + API key interceptor |
| `src/api/movies.ts` | `getTrending`, `getTopRated`, `searchMovies`, `getMovieDetail` |
| `src/api/types.ts` | `Movie`, `MovieDetail`, `Genre`, `PaginatedResponse` interfaces |
| `src/hooks/useHome.ts` | Fetches trending + top-rated in parallel |
| `src/hooks/useSearch.ts` | Debounced search (400ms) |
| `src/hooks/useMovieDetail.ts` | Fetches single movie detail by ID |
| `src/navigation/types.ts` | `RootTabParamList`, `HomeStackParamList`, `SearchStackParamList` |
| `src/navigation/RootNavigator.tsx` | Bottom tabs (Home, Search, Watchlist, Profile) with nested stacks for Detail |
| `src/screens/HomeScreen.tsx` | Uses `useHome` hook |
| `src/screens/SearchScreen.tsx` | Uses `useSearch` hook with text input |
| `src/screens/DetailScreen.tsx` | Uses `useMovieDetail` hook, receives `movieId` via route params |
| `src/screens/WatchlistScreen.tsx` | Reads from zustand watchlist store |
| `src/screens/ProfileScreen.tsx` | Static placeholder |
| `src/store/watchlistStore.ts` | Zustand store with AsyncStorage persistence |
| `src/utils/image.ts` | TMDB image URL helper |
| `src/components/common/.gitkeep` | Placeholder for shared components |

### Root Config Files Created
- `.env.example` -- placeholder `TMDB_API_KEY`
- `docs/ADR.md` -- 5 architectural decisions documented
- `prompt-logs/.gitkeep` -- empty placeholder
- `react-native.config.js` -- font asset linking config

### Files Modified
- `App.tsx` -- Rewired to `NavigationContainer` + `RootNavigator` with dark theme using your color tokens
- `.gitignore` -- Added `.env` and `.env.local` entries
- `package.json` -- Updated by npm install with all new dependencies

### Next Steps
- Download **Manrope** and **Inter** `.ttf` files from Google Fonts and place them in `assets/fonts/`, then run `npx react-native-asset` to link them
- Create a `.env` file with your actual TMDB API key (never commit it)
- Run `cd ios && pod install` to link the new native dependencies on iOS

_**Agent (claude-4.6-opus-high-thinking)**_

The local_repo exists with the `org/` directory containing the artifact. The fix is in place. Here's what changed:

**File modified:** `android/build.gradle`

Added an `allprojects` block that points Gradle to the local Maven repository bundled inside `@react-native-async-storage/async-storage`. AsyncStorage v3 ships `org.asyncstorage.shared_storage:storage-android` as a local artifact rather than publishing it to Maven Central, so this manual repo declaration is required.

```8:30:android/build.gradle
    }
    repositories {
        google()
        mavenCentral()
    }
    dependencies {
        classpath("com.android.tools.build:gradle")
        classpath("com.facebook.react:react-native-gradle-plugin")
        classpath("org.jetbrains.kotlin:kotlin-gradle-plugin")
    }
}

allprojects {
    repositories {
        maven {
            url = uri(rootProject.file("../node_modules/@react-native-async-storage/async-storage/android/local_repo"))
        }
    }
}

apply plugin: "com.facebook.react.rootproject"
```

Run `npx react-native run-android` again -- the build should now resolve the AsyncStorage dependency successfully.

_**Agent (claude-4.6-opus-high-thinking)**_

Here's how fonts are set up in this app -- it's a **three-layer** system:

---

### Layer 1: Font Asset Linking (`react-native.config.js`)

```1:7:react-native.config.js
module.exports = {
  project: {
    ios: {},
    android: {},
  },
  assets: ['./assets/fonts/'],
};
```

This tells React Native's asset linker to copy any `.ttf` / `.otf` files from `assets/fonts/` into the native Android and iOS font directories. When you run `npx react-native-asset`, it copies the font files into:
- **Android:** `android/app/src/main/assets/fonts/`
- **iOS:** Adds them to the Xcode project and `Info.plist`

**Current status:** The `assets/fonts/` directory is empty -- the actual `.ttf` files for **Manrope** and **Inter** haven't been downloaded yet. This is a required step before fonts will render.

---

### Layer 2: Typography Tokens (`src/theme/typography.ts`)

This is where every font style in the app is defined as a reusable token:

```3:51:src/theme/typography.ts
export const typography = {
  display_lg: {
    fontFamily: 'Manrope-ExtraBold',
    fontSize: 56,
    fontWeight: '800',
    letterSpacing: -0.02 * 56,
  } satisfies TextStyle,
  // ... more tokens
  body_md: {
    fontFamily: 'Inter-Regular',
    fontSize: 14,
    fontWeight: '400',
    letterSpacing: 0,
  } satisfies TextStyle,
  // ...
} as const;
```

The naming convention matters. In React Native, `fontFamily` must match the **file name** (without extension) of the `.ttf` file. So you need files named exactly:
- `Manrope-ExtraBold.ttf`
- `Manrope-Bold.ttf`
- `Manrope-SemiBold.ttf`
- `Inter-Regular.ttf`
- `Inter-SemiBold.ttf`

Screens then spread these tokens into their `StyleSheet`:

```typescript
const styles = StyleSheet.create({
  heading: {
    ...typography.headline_md,   // spreads fontFamily, fontSize, fontWeight, letterSpacing
    color: colors.on_surface,
  },
});
```

This keeps font definitions centralized -- no screen ever hardcodes a `fontFamily` or `fontSize` directly.

---

### Layer 3: Navigation Theme Fonts (`App.tsx`)

```23:28:App.tsx
          fonts: {
            regular: {fontFamily: 'Inter-Regular', fontWeight: '400'},
            medium: {fontFamily: 'Inter-Medium', fontWeight: '500'},
            bold: {fontFamily: 'Manrope-Bold', fontWeight: '700'},
            heavy: {fontFamily: 'Manrope-ExtraBold', fontWeight: '800'},
          },
```

React Navigation renders its own text elements (header titles, tab labels, back button text). This `fonts` object in the navigation theme tells it which font families to use for those built-in elements, so they match the rest of the app.

---

### What you still need to do

The `assets/fonts/` folder is empty. To complete the font setup:

1. Download **Manrope** (ExtraBold, Bold, SemiBold weights) and **Inter** (Regular, Medium, SemiBold weights) `.ttf` files from [Google Fonts](https://fonts.google.com/)
2. Place them in `assets/fonts/` with exact names like `Manrope-ExtraBold.ttf`, `Inter-Regular.ttf`, etc.
3. Run `npx react-native-asset` to link them into the native projects
4. Rebuild the app (`npx react-native run-android`)

Until the font files are present, React Native will silently fall back to the system default font.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The user is getting an authentication error when trying to push to GitHub. The error messages tell us:

1. `fatal: cannot run .../askpass.sh: No such file or directory` - Cursor's git askpass helper is missing
2. `remote: Invalid username or token. Password authentication is not supported for Git operations.` - GitHub no longer supports password authentication for HTTPS

The key issue is #2: GitHub deprecated password authentication in August 2021. You need to use either:
- A **Personal Access Token (PAT)** instead of a password
- **SSH keys** for authentication

I should explain this clearly without making any changes since I'm being asked "why".</details></think>

_**Agent (claude-4.6-opus-high-thinking)**_

Two separate issues are shown in that output:

### 1. GitHub no longer accepts passwords

This is the main blocker:

```
remote: Invalid username or token. Password authentication is not supported for Git operations.
```

GitHub removed password-based HTTPS authentication. You need to use one of these instead:

- **Personal Access Token (PAT):** Go to [GitHub Settings > Developer settings > Personal access tokens > Tokens (classic)](https://github.com/settings/tokens), generate a token with `repo` scope, and use that token as the password when prompted.

- **SSH (recommended):** Set up an SSH key and switch the remote URL to SSH format:
  ```bash
  git remote set-url origin git@github.com:modishubham/streamlist.git
  ```

### 2. Cursor's askpass helper not found

```
fatal: cannot run /tmp/.mount_Cursorb8nrJL/usr/share/cursor/resources/app/extensions/git/dist/askpass.sh: No such file or directory
```

This is a secondary issue -- Cursor's AppImage mount path changed or expired, so git can't find the credential prompt helper. You can work around this by unsetting the environment variable before pushing:

```bash
unset GIT_ASKPASS
git push origin main
```

**Recommended fix:** Set up SSH authentication. Generate a key if you don't have one (`ssh-keygen -t ed25519`), add the public key to your [GitHub SSH keys](https://github.com/settings/keys), then switch the remote to SSH format. This avoids password/token prompts entirely.
